2606.19888.title: SL-S4Wave: Self-Supervised Learning of Physiological Waveforms with Structured State Space Models
2606.19888.paper_summary: Modeling long-sequence medical time series data, such as electrocardiograms (ECG), poses significant challenges due to high sampling rates, multichannel signal complexity, inherent noise, and limited labeled data. While recent self-supervised learning (SSL) methods, based on various encoder architectures such as convolutional neural networks, have been proposed to learn representations from unlabeled data, they often fall short in capturing long-range dependencies and noise-invariant features. Structured state space models (S4) excel at long-sequence modeling, but existing S4 architectures fail to capture the unique characteristics of multichannel physiological waveforms. In this work, we propose SL-S4Wave, a self-supervised learning framework that combines contrastive learning with a tailored encoder built on structured state space models. The encoder incorporates multi-layer global convolution using multiscale subkernels, enabling the capture of both fine-grained local patterns and long-range temporal dependencies in noisy, high-resolution multichannel waveforms. Extensive experiments on real-world datasets demonstrate that SL-S4Wave (1) consistently outperforms state-of-the-art supervised and self-supervised baselines in a challenging arrhythmia detection task, (2) achieves high performance with significantly fewer labeled examples, showcasing strong label efficiency, and (3) maintains robust performance on long waveform segments, highlighting its capacity to model complex temporal dynamics in long sequences that most existing approaches fail to efficiently model, and (4) transfers effectively to unseen arrhythmia types, underscoring its robust cross-domain generalization. We additionally evaluate SL-S4Wave on multiple EEG tasks, achieving superior performance over strong baselines, demonstrating generalizability of our approach beyond cardiac waveforms.
2606.19888.paper_summary_zh: 建模長序列醫療時間序列數據，例如心電圖（ECG），由於高取樣率、多通道信號的複雜性、固有噪聲和有限的標記數據，面臨重大挑戰。雖然最近基於各種編碼器架構（如卷積神經網絡）的自監督學習（SSL）方法已被提出以從未標記數據中學習表示，但它們在捕捉長距依賴性和抗噪聲特徵方面往往不夠理想。結構化狀態空間模型（S4）在長序列建模方面表現出色，但現有的S4架構未能捕捉多通道生理波形的獨特特徵。在本研究中，我們提出SL-S4Wave，一種自監督學習框架，將對比學習與基於結構化狀態空間模型的定制編碼器相結合。該編碼器使用多尺度子核進行多層全局卷積，能夠捕捉高噪聲、高解析度多通道波形中的精細局部模式和長距時間依賴性。在真實世界數據集上的廣泛實驗表明，SL-S4Wave（1）在具有挑戰性的心律失常檢測任務中，始終超越最先進的監督和自監督基準，(2) 在顯著較少的標記樣本下達到高性能，展示出強大的標記效率，(3) 在長波形段上保持穩健的性能，突顯其建模複雜時間動態的能力，這是大多數現有方法無法有效建模的，並且(4) 能夠有效轉移到未見的心律失常類型，強調其強大的跨領域泛化能力。我們還在多個腦電圖任務上評估SL-S4Wave，取得了超越強基準的優越性能，展示了我們的方法在心臟波形之外的可泛化性。
2606.19888.author: Feng Wu et.al.
2606.19888.authors: Feng Wu, Harsh Deep, Eric Lehman, Sanyam Kapoor, Guoshuai Zhao, Rahul Krishnan, Gari Clifford, Li-wei H Lehman
